definition: The bifurcation between stagflationary and disinflationary downturn paths is the most consequential portfolio construction variable, with radically different correlation signatures: stagflationary (positive stock-bond correlation, both falling) vs disinflationary (negative stock-bond correlation, bonds hedge). Tariff/de-globalization trajectory is the key swing variable.
evidence: Scenario probabilities: 45% extended mid-cycle, 30% disinflationary, 15% stagflationary, 10% re-acceleration
Stagflationary path is worst for 60/40 portfolios
Tariff/de-globalization trajectory is genuinely unprecedented with limited analogue applicability
pair_0 identified this framework but noted probability assignments are subjective
open_questions: What is the probability-weighted optimal portfolio allocation across these scenarios?
How quickly can the regime shift from one path to another?
Are tariff scenarios modelable given their unprecedented nature?
